title: GETBIT
description: Returns a bit value by offset.

jedis.signature: boolean getbit(final byte[] key, final long offset)

returns.description: Long integer-reply the bit value stored at offset.
Long integer-reply the bit value stored at offset.
Long integer-reply the bit value stored at offset.

go-redis.signature: GetBit(ctx context.Context, key string, offset int64)

params.description: The offset in the string to get a bit at.
params.name: offset

content:
Returns the bit value at _offset_ in the string value stored at _key_. When _offset_ is beyond the string length, the string is assumed to be a contiguous space with 0 bits. When _key_ does not exist it is assumed to be an empty string, so _offset_ is always out of range and the value is also assumed to be a contiguous space with 0 bits. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SETBIT mykey 7 1 GETBIT mykey 0 GETBIT mykey 7 GETBIT mykey 100 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="getbit-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} The bit value stored at _offset_, one of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0`. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1`. -tab-sep- The bit value stored at _offset_, one of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0`. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1`. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SETBIT mykey 7 1
GETBIT mykey 0
GETBIT mykey 7
GETBIT mykey 100
intro:
Returns the bit value at _offset_ in the string value stored at _key_.

When _offset_ is beyond the string length, the string is assumed to be a
contiguous space with 0 bits.
When _key_ does not exist it is assumed to be an empty string, so _offset_ is
always out of range and the value is also assumed to be a contiguous space with
0 bits.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns the bit value at _offset_ in the string value stored at _key_.

When _offset_ is beyond the string length, the string is assumed to be a
contiguous space with 0 bits.
When _key_ does not exist it is assumed to be an empty string, so _offset_ is
always out of range and the value is also assumed to be a contiguous space with
0 bits.

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="getbit-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

The bit value stored at _offset_, one of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0`.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1`.

-tab-sep-

The bit value stored at _offset_, one of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0`.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1`.

{{< /multitabs >}}
